narration: Your employer is handing you free money with a 100% guaranteed return — and one in three workers refuses to take it. Here's why that's insane.
visual: Person silhouette standing next to a glowing green money bag labeled '$2,820'. Question mark above person's head. '100% RETURN' pulsing text. Dark background.

narration: It's called the 401k employer match. Every dollar you put in up to 4.7% of your salary, your company doubles — instantly. That's a 100% return before the market does anything.
visual: Two stacked bars side-by-side: 'YOUR MONEY' and 'EMPLOYER MATCH' (equal size, green), with '= 100% INSTANT RETURN' badge appearing. Light background.

narration: On a $60,000 salary, that's $2,820 in free employer money every single year. Most people leave every dollar of it sitting on the table.
visual: Salary bar '$60K' with a 4.7% slice highlighted. '$2,820 FREE' stamp springs in below in green. 'PER YEAR' label. Dark background.

narration: Over 30 years, that $2,820 a year grows to $375,000 at normal market returns. That's a quarter of your retirement — just from the free match.
visual: Growth bar chart from Year 0 to Year 30, bar rising to $375K label at top. Two-tone bar: your contributions (dark green) vs employer match (bright green). Light background.

narration: Wild, right? One in three eligible workers don't contribute enough to capture the full match. They're leaving tens of thousands on the table every decade.
visual: Three person silhouettes in a row. Two are green with a checkmark and money bags. One is red, walking away from a pile of cash. '1 IN 3 REFUSE' label. Dark background.

narration: The rule is dead simple — always contribute at least enough to grab every dollar of your employer match. It's the only guaranteed 100% return on earth. Don't leave it.
visual: Checklist: person checking off 'Contribute to match' box. Piggy bank growing larger. '$375,000' target badge springs in. 'TAKE THE FREE MONEY' CTA. Light background.
